behaviors.description: The conversation history passed to the LLM is trimmed to the most recent 10 messages when the full history exceeds the context window.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is mainly handoff/orchestration (history builder -> LLM request composer), not just a pure helper. REAL-INFRA: no explicit real OS/network/subprocess requirement in the behavior text, so System is not required. LLM-DEP: correctness does not depend on model judgment; only on payload shape. Rejected higher level: no text requiring real external contract or model-quality validation.
behaviors.test_description: Run the chatbot service with in-process fakes for DB/LLM transport, provide >10-message history, and assert the actual request payload sent by the orchestration layer contains only the latest 10 messages in order.

behaviors.description: The generated response addresses the customer's question rather than producing a generic or off-topic reply.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: this is explicitly model output quality/relevance. A stubbed response would hide the real failure mode. Rejected Workflow: behavior states a single response-quality judgment, not a required multi-agentic sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Use real LLM calls on a representative prompt set and evaluate whether answers stay on-topic and question-directed (with rubric or human/LLM judge), tracking pass rate across repeated runs.

behaviors.description: The tone re-check correctly identifies when a response is too formal for a casual-tier customer and triggers regeneration.
behaviors.justification: This behavior chains multiple agentic steps: initial generation -> tone judgment -> regeneration path. Correctness depends on real model judgment ('correctly identifies too formal') plus sequenced agentic control flow. Rejected Agentic-only: text explicitly includes the downstream regeneration action, making this a multi-step agentic pipeline check.
behaviors.test_description: Execute end-to-end with real LLM(s) for both generation and tone-check, using casual-tier cases likely to provoke over-formal drafts; verify the first draft is flagged, regeneration is actually invoked, and final delivered output comes from the regenerated step.

behaviors.description: The full exchange (user message, generated response, any regenerations) is persisted to the database with correct timestamps.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is in module interaction (chat flow -> persistence schema/transaction), not single-function logic. REAL-INFRA: behavior does not explicitly require real external API/OS/subprocess contract checks. LLM-DEP: can be validated with deterministic stubs; output quality is irrelevant. Rejected System: no explicit external boundary contract requirement beyond local persistence.
behaviors.test_description: Run service + real local test DB, execute scenarios with and without regeneration, then read back persisted rows and assert all exchange entries exist with expected ordering and timestamp fields.

behaviors.description: When the tone check triggers regeneration, the second response replaces the first — the customer never sees the rejected draft.
behaviors.justification: SYSTEM TRIAD #3 applies: must prove externally visible side effect ('customer never sees rejected draft') via round-trip observation at delivery boundary. Integration fakes can miss leaks in real delivery path. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: this behavior focuses on visibility/replacement semantics, not quality of model decisions; trigger can be forced deterministically.
behaviors.test_description: Drive a real request through the customer-facing interface (e.g., HTTP/chat endpoint), force tone-trigger path, and verify returned/streamed customer-visible content includes only regenerated text while internal logs may retain rejected draft metadata.
